prompt:
Every night our reporting job hands the analytics team a .sql seed file they load into whichever warehouse their project uses — some are Postgres-flavored, some MySQL. The current emitter is string concatenation, and it produces broken files whenever the data is even slightly interesting: staff named O'Brien split a literal in half, a note field with a real newline is fine but a value that IS the four letters NULL gets written unquoted and loads as a missing value, and badge photos (raw bytes) come out as [object Object]. I'm done patching it — write a correct emitter, sqlemit.ts, no dependencies. `node --test test_sqlemit.ts` is acceptance; the test file is read-only.

API and rules:
- type Dialect = 'ansi' | 'mysql'.
- quoteIdent(name, dialect): ansi wraps in double quotes doubling any embedded double quote; mysql wraps in backticks doubling any embedded backtick. The other dialect's quote character is ordinary text. Empty name throws TypeError.
- renderLiteral(v) for v: string | number | boolean | null | Uint8Array. Strings: wrap in single quotes and double every embedded single quote — that is the ONLY transformation; backslashes and newlines pass through untouched (these files load with standard-conforming strings). null renders as unquoted NULL; the STRING 'NULL' stays a quoted literal — mixing those up is exactly the bug we keep shipping. Numbers must be finite (else RangeError) and render via String(v). Booleans render TRUE / FALSE. Uint8Array renders as X'..' with uppercase two-digit hex per byte, X'' when empty. Anything else throws TypeError.
- renderInsert(table, columns, rows, dialect) returns exactly:

  INSERT INTO <ident> (<ident>, <ident>) VALUES\n  (<lit>, <lit>),\n  (<lit>, <lit>);

  — header line, then each row on its own line indented two spaces, ', ' between items, ',' after every row except the last which gets ';'. No trailing newline. Empty columns, empty rows, or any row whose length differs from columns: throw Error.
- writeReport(path, tables, dialect) where tables is [{table, columns, rows}, ...]: write the statements joined by one blank line, with a final trailing newline, UTF-8, LF only. No tables writes an empty file. The tests byte-compare the emitted file, so exact means exact.
notes: Authoring-time toolchain: node 26.3. Differentiation from ts-sqlplan (parses/plans SQL) and ts-jsonquote (JSON encoder): this renders SQL literals/identifiers and emits a byte-compared .sql file. Correctness framing per the 5E hygiene rule.
